name: AWS Certified AI Practitioner (AIF-C01)
description: Foundational certification validating understanding of AI/ML concepts and generative AI on AWS. For individuals in any role seeking to demonstrate AI literacy. Covers AI fundamentals, generative AI foundations, applications of foundation models, responsible AI, and security of AI solutions. 85 questions, 120 minutes.
exportIntroQuestions:
## About this document

This document is a collection of practice exam questions for the **AWS Certified AI Practitioner (AIF-C01)** certification. The questions were selected because they represent foundational AI and generative AI concepts that every practitioner should understand when working with AI services on AWS.

### Certification context

This certification validates overall understanding of AI, ML, and generative AI on AWS, including topics such as:

* Core AI and machine learning concepts (supervised, unsupervised, reinforcement learning)
* Generative AI fundamentals (transformers, tokens, embeddings, prompt engineering, RAG)
* Applications of foundation models and AWS generative AI services (Bedrock, SageMaker, Q)
* Responsible AI practices, bias detection, and limitations of AI systems
* Security, compliance, and governance for AI solutions

### About the questions

Each question in this document is independent and should be analyzed as a standalone scenario. Practice exams intentionally present isolated situations, and questions may appear in any order during the actual certification exam. Information from one question should never be assumed to apply to another.

For each scenario, this document includes:

* The original practice exam question.
* An explanation of the reasoning behind the correct answer.
* A discussion of why the remaining options are less appropriate for the stated requirements.

### Learning objective

The primary goal of this material is to develop a solid understanding of AI and generative AI concepts rather than memorization. The focus should be on understanding the fundamental principles, identifying the appropriate AWS AI services for common use cases, and recognizing the responsible AI considerations that guide implementation decisions.

By studying the reasoning behind each answer, readers can build the foundational AI knowledge expected for the AWS AI Practitioner certification exam.
exportIntroTranscripts:
## About this document

This document contains technical summaries of lesson transcripts related to the **AWS Certified AI Practitioner (AIF-C01)** certification.

### Purpose
Organizes and synthesizes lesson content in a structured format for quick review and deeper understanding of the concepts presented.
exportIntroChat:
## About this document

This document contains AI study conversations related to the **AWS Certified AI Practitioner (AIF-C01)** certification.

### Purpose
Records discussions and explanations generated during interactive AI study sessions, serving as complementary reference material.

domains.name: Fundamentals of AI and ML
domains.description: Domain 1 (20%): Explain basic AI/ML concepts, identify practical use cases for AI, describe the ML development lifecycle.

domains.name: Fundamentals of Generative AI
domains.description: Domain 2 (24%): Explain foundation models, understand generative AI concepts (transformers, tokens, embeddings, prompt engineering, fine-tuning, RAG).

domains.name: Applications of Foundation Models
domains.description: Domain 3 (28%): Describe design considerations for foundation model applications, choose effective prompt engineering techniques, identify AWS services for generative AI (Bedrock, SageMaker, Q).

domains.name: Guidelines for Responsible AI
domains.description: Domain 4 (14%): Explain responsible AI practices, recognize limitations and biases, describe AWS tools for responsible AI.

domains.name: Security, Compliance, and Governance
domains.description: Domain 5 (14%): Identify AWS services and features for securing AI solutions, describe data governance, understand compliance requirements.
